summary.claim_boundary: These invariants test declared score behavior and fail-closed routing properties. They do not prove production incident reduction or customer-calibrated thresholds.

results.description: Higher reversibility must not increase irreversible exposure and must not reduce reversible capacity or authorization score.
results.expectation: irreversible_exposure_score <= prior value; reversible_capacity_score >= prior value; risk_adjusted_authorization_score >= prior value

results.description: Higher rollback latency must not reduce exposure or stress and must not increase recovery capacity or authorization score.
results.expectation: irreversible_exposure_score >= prior value; operational_stress_score >= prior value; reversible_capacity_score <= prior value; risk_adjusted_authorization_score <= prior value

results.description: Lower evidence validity must not increase confidence or authorization and must not reduce operational stress.
results.expectation: confidence_score <= prior value; risk_adjusted_authorization_score <= prior value; operational_stress_score >= prior value

results.description: Higher anomaly pressure must not increase confidence and must not reduce operational stress.
results.expectation: confidence_score <= prior value; operational_stress_score >= prior value

results.description: External side effects and sensitive data must not reduce irreversible exposure.
A data-boundary violation must block an executor even when capability and reliability scores are strong.
A tool-authority gap must block an executor even when general capability scores are strong.
A required-capability gap must block an executor despite low cost and latency.
Higher task risk must not increase the risk-adjusted executor score for the same candidate set.
results.expectation: risk_pressure >= prior value; risk_adjusted_executor_score <= prior value

results.description: Higher safety history must not reduce reliability, recoverability, or model fitness for an otherwise identical qualified executor.
results.expectation: recoverability_fit >= prior value; reliability_fit >= prior value; model_fitness_score >= prior value
